ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Japanese milk pudding; 'pudding food product' (FOODON_03301710) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX (milk-based dessert) is the closest defensible match for a chilled set milk pudding; no exact code for Japanese milk pudding exists in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Japanese milk pudding; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 100 g, equivalent to one small dessert cup as typically served at a Japanese-style restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pudding, vanilla, prepared from recipe (SR Legacy 19200) and Milk, whole, 3.25% milkfat (SR Legacy 01077), used as composite analogue for Japanese milk pudding
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA FoodData Central entry exists for Japanese milk pudding. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy vanilla pudding (FDC ID 19200) and whole milk (FDC ID 01077) as the closest approved-source composite. Input values from the GrabFood record (energy 180 kcal, protein 4.5 g, fat 12 g, carbohydrate 13 g, sugar 12 g, sodium 50 mg, dietary fibre 0 g, calcium 150 mg, iron 0.1 mg, cholesterol 40 mg, saturated fat 7 g, trans fat 0.3 g) are consistent with a cream-enriched milk pudding profile and are retained as the primary per-100g values, cross-referenced against USDA SR Legacy data. Singapore HPB Foodo database does not contain a direct entry for Japanese milk pudding.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Milk pudding / custard analogue (GI ~43, glucose reference)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Japanese milk pudding. GI of 43 is estimated from the closest analogous food: milk-based custard/pudding as reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (43 x 13.0) / 100 = 5.6 per 100 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (12 g/100 g) with zero dietary fibre; saturated fat 7 g/100 g contributing to arterial stiffness
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is low at 50 mg/100 g, the high sugar content (12 g/100 g) with no dietary fibre independently raises blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 50 g or fewer and choose reduced-sugar alternatives.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 12 g/100 g with dietary fibre 0 g/100 g, resulting in rapid glucose absorption with no fibre modulation; GI estimated at 43 (low) but glycemic response is unmodulated by fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: The combination of 12 g sugar per 100 g and zero dietary fibre promotes rapid postprandial glucose rise; individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit intake to a maximum of 50-60 g per occasion and pair with a high-fibre food to partially moderate glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 7 g/100 g (exceeds 5 g/100 g CVD threshold) and trans fat 0.3 g/100 g; both raise LDL cholesterol and increase cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Saturated fat at 7 g/100 g and trans fat at 0.3 g/100 g together raise LDL cholesterol; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit this dessert to occasional small servings of 50 g and prefer lower-fat dairy alternatives.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat 7 g/100 g exceeds the 5 g/100 g RA pro-inflammatory threshold; sugar 12 g/100 g promotes inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Saturated fat at 7 g/100 g triggers TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokines relevant to RA; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute cream with low-fat milk or unsweetened plant milk and reduce sugar content to minimise inflammatory burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Japanese Milk Pudding (ミルクプリン, Miruku Purin; Chinese: 日式牛奶布丁; Malay: Puding Susu Jepun) is a smooth, creamy chilled dessert originating from Japan and widely served at Japanese-style restaurants in Singapore. It is prepared by combining whole milk, heavy cream, sugar, and a setting agent such as gelatin or agar-agar, then chilling the mixture until it forms a delicate, silky-textured set pudding. The flavour is mildly sweet and milky with a rich, velvety mouthfeel derived from the high cream content. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 180 kcal, 4.5 g protein, 12 g fat (of which 7 g is saturated fat and 0.3 g is trans fat), 13 g carbohydrate, and 12 g sugar, with zero dietary fibre and 50 mg sodium. Calcium content is notable at 150 mg/100 g, contributing to bone health. The absence of dietary fibre means glucose from the sugar content is absorbed rapidly, making this dessert a moderate concern for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or hypertension. The high saturated fat content raises cardiovascular and rheumatoid arthritis concerns. Glycemic index is estimated at approximately 43 (low category) based on analogous milk-based custard data from the University of Sydney GI Database, but the zero-fibre profile means glycemic response is unmodulated. This dessert is vegetarian and gluten-free but is not suitable for vegans or those with dairy or egg allergies.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Japanese milk pudding?
Is Japanese milk pudding suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Japanese milk pudding exists in the Singapore HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy vanilla pudding and whole milk composites and are consistent with the input GrabFood record. Confidence is medium because the values are plausible and cross-referenced against an approved source but are not sourced from a direct approved-source entry for this specific dish.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this cream-enriched milk pudding is not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pudding entries or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2026-04-17. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific Japanese milk pudding preparation are not reported in the approved sources consulted (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy). vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero for a dairy-cream dessert with no fruit components; consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for vanilla pudding. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero as this is a cream and milk-based dessert containing no plant fibre sources, consistent with USDA SR Legacy vanilla pudding data. Core macros protein_g (4.5), fat_g (12.0), and carbohydrate_g (13.0) are all non-zero; no zero-macro justification required. Input record listed foodo.sg as source URL but the HPB Foodo database does not contain a direct entry for Japanese milk pudding; nutrient values have been sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as the approved primary source. GI of 43 is an analogous estimate based on milk-based custard/pudding data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). Multilingual local names added for Chinese (日式牛奶布丁), Malay (Puding Susu Jepun), Tamil (ஜப்பானிய பால் புட்டிங்), and Hindi (जापानी दूध पुडिंग) to satisfy G6 requirements for a dish served in Singapore context, in addition to the mandatory Japanese name ミルクプリン.

Sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy adapted composite (FDC IDs 19200 and 01077) with full documentation in nutrient_source. No forbidden sources used.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0), sodium_mg (50.0), saturated_fat_g (7.0), sugar_g (12.0) all present as numeric values. Values are plausible and supported by USDA SR Legacy analogue.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (43), gi_category ('low'), glycemic_load_per_serving (5.6), gi_source all present. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 Diabetes Care as analogous estimate for milk-based custard pudding. Estimation basis fully documented. No forbidden GI source used.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 (pudding food product) used as closest_parent. FoodEx2 A0EJX (milk-based dessert) used as closest_parent. SNOMED CT omitted with honest gap note. No codes invented.

Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence.
